Comment and analysis on the big stories of the day in Ireland and abroad with a panel of guests covering the key issues.
Gordon and Frankie end up in a dangerous predicament when they aid an injured trawler-man on board a sinking boat, and Matron resigns to start a new life.
Loose Women as they chat about news, issues and life in general.
Brian McFadden presents the dining challenge show. Superbike champion and King of the Jungle Carl Fogarty plays mystery dinner party host to four strangers.
Ekin-Su Culculoglu, Hugh Dennis, Kelly Hoppen and Shaun Wright-Phillips attempt to impress their loved ones and mentors to avoid the cook-off.
Jeff goes to the rescue of pot-holers in a cave and takes the opportunity to set the record straight about himself and restore his reputation.
Gordon and Frankie end up in a dangerous predicament when they aid an injured trawler-man on board a sinking boat, and Matron resigns to start a new life.
Some of the top celebrities across Ireland are interviewed while the hosts feature knowledgeable advice on topics that cover multiple topics and industries.
Ainsley is joined by chefs Poppy and Sam in the kitchen garden of the Wimpole Estate, Cambridge for an al fresco cook off.
An examination of the top news and entertainment events of 2000 set to music.
Two planes hit the Twin Towers in New York; the foot-and-mouth outbreak; Eamon Dunphy tries television presenting.
Alan Quinlan speaks about dealing with anxiety and other mental health strains during his career, which featured several highlight moments as well.
An activist, a barrister, a renowned pundit, Joe Brolly opens up in a rare and emotional interview about his life, his trauma, and his football controversies.
Gift wrapped is a fun quiz which sees four couples compete to win their perfect gift list. Each couple builds a unique list by selecting their own personal dream prizes.
A marketing manager judges who has the best sense of style between a roofer, a physiotherapist and a young Essex man then takes the winner on a date.
It's judgement day as the six remaining chefs fight it out for two places in the final. With a closely fought battle they celebrate with one last night in Barcelona.
Four strangers answer trivia questions against quizzer who stands against them.
Presided over by outspoken Judge Judith Sheindlin; with her no-nonsense, decisive approach to justice; she has established herself as a pop-culture icon for generations of viewers.
A desperate woman's desire to have a family leads to making some poor choices; a teen wants payback for a ticket after her boyfriend cheated.
A mix of celebrity interviews, entertainment news, current affairs discussions, a look at sports competitions, health columns, cooking tips and tricks, and more.

Following some of European football's top officials as they adapt to the introduction of VAR, giving their frank assessments of some of the most-talked-about decisions.

Ainsley is joined by Georgina Hayden and Joseph Denison Carey at Blickling Hall in Norfolk for a light-hearted cooking contest.
Roy Keane and Mick McCarthy have their famous disagreement; Bertie Ahern leads Fianna Fail to victory in the general election.
A series which chronicles the news and events of the past 20 years, all set to music. This episode looks back at the year 2003.

The former Republic of Ireland midfielder talks about his life in professional football and his battles with addiction.
Three engaged couples get the chance to win their wedding gift list and a dream holiday. Quiz show, hosted by Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford.
A French bulldog with walking problems is brought in to receive specialised spinal surgery; a wild seal requires treatment to save its eye.
It's the final service, one chef will be crowned Star Chef. Who's got what it takes? And when the doors close, will love still be dish of the day?
